results.resolved_title: Protein Secretion Systems in Pseudomonas aeruginosa: An Essay on Diversity, Evolution, and Function
results.quote:
ToxA (PA1948)

Exotoxin A-ADP ribosyltransferase
results.note: Listed in Table as a T2SS (Xcp) substrate of P. aeruginosa. Review gives ToxA locus PA1948; batch uses PA1148, same exotoxin A.

results.resolved_title: Protein Secretion Systems in Pseudomonas aeruginosa: An Essay on Diversity, Evolution, and Function
results.quote: resulted in the periplasmic accumulation of otherwise secreted enzymes such as elastase (LasB; Bever and Iglewski,), lipase (LipA; Wohlfarth et al.,)
results.note: Elastase LasB is a T2SS (Xcp) substrate; also in substrate Table (PA3724).

results.resolved_title: Protein Secretion Systems in Pseudomonas aeruginosa: An Essay on Diversity, Evolution, and Function
results.quote: such as elastase (LasB; Bever and Iglewski,), lipase (LipA; Wohlfarth et al.,), alkaline phosphatase (PhoA; Filloux et al.,)
results.note: Lipase LipA is a T2SS (Xcp) substrate; also in Table (PA2862).

results.resolved_title: Protein Secretion Systems in Pseudomonas aeruginosa: An Essay on Diversity, Evolution, and Function
results.quote: lipase (LipA; Wohlfarth et al.,), alkaline phosphatase (PhoA; Filloux et al.,), or phospholipase C (PlcH; Wretlind and Pavlovskis,
results.note: Alkaline phosphatase PhoA is a T2SS (Xcp) substrate; also in Table (PA3296).

results.quote: achieves the secretion of all Xcp substrates but the aminopeptidase PaAP (Michel et al.,). This is another indication of a specific interaction between the XcpP/XcpQ-like subcomplex and T2SS substrates.
results.note: PaAP is a T2SS (Xcp) aminopeptidase substrate (PA2939).

results.resolved_title: EspH, a new cytoskeleton-modulating effector of enterohaemorrhagic and enteropathogenic Escherichia coli
results.quote: EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 12535063). EspH is a LEE/TTSS effector of both EHEC and EPEC; covers EHEC O157:H7 row (ECs4564).

results.resolved_title: EspH, a new cytoskeleton-modulating effector of enterohaemorrhagic and enteropathogenic Escherichia coli
results.quote: EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 12535063). Same paper covers EPEC E2348/69 row (E2348C_3944).

results.resolved_title: A Type VI Secretion System Trans-Kingdom Effector Is Required for the Delivery of a Novel Antibacterial Toxin in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
results.quote: we have discovered a novel antibacterial toxin, Tle3, and its cognate immunity, Tli3, whose genes are encoded downstream of. By characterizing the secretion mechanism of Tle3 by H2-T6SS
results.note: Tle3 (PA0260) is an antibacterial effector secreted by the H2-T6SS of P. aeruginosa.

results.resolved_title: A Type VI Secretion System Trans-Kingdom Effector Is Required for the Delivery of a Novel Antibacterial Toxin in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
results.quote: this did not lead to immunodetection of Tla3in the extracellular medium although Hcp2b was readily observed... Tla3 is thus not an effector.
results.note: Tla3 (PA0259) is a cytoplasmic adaptor/chaperone that loads Tle3 onto VgrG2b; paper shows it is NOT secreted and explicitly 'not an effector'.

results.resolved_title: A type VI secretion system effector delivery mechanism dependent on PAAR and a chaperone-co-chaperone complex
results.quote: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa PAO1 TOX-REase-5 domain-containing effector TseT directly interacts with PAAR4 and the chaperone TecT for delivery
results.note: Abstract (PMID 29632369). TecT (PA3905) is described as the chaperone for TseT delivery, not as a secreted effector itself.

results.resolved_title: A type VI secretion system effector delivery mechanism dependent on PAAR and a chaperone-co-chaperone complex
results.quote: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa PAO1 TOX-REase-5 domain-containing effector TseT directly interacts with PAAR4 and the chaperone TecT for delivery, and an immunity protein, TsiT, for protection from its toxicity.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 29632369). TseT (PA3907) is a P. aeruginosa T6SS effector delivered via PAAR4.

results.resolved_title: Translocator proteins in the two-partner secretion family have multiple domains
results.quote: the periplasmic domain is required for secretion of HMW1 and that the C-terminal membrane anchor (HMW1B-(234-545)) is capable of oligomerization and pore formation.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 16648638). HMW1 is the TpsA exoprotein secreted via the two-partner (TPS / T5b) system by its cognate TpsB HMW1B in H. influenzae.

results.resolved_title: Translocator proteins in the two-partner secretion family have multiple domains
results.note: No PMC full text; abstract (PMID 16648638) discusses only HMW1/HMW1B, never HMW2/HMW2A. Cannot confirm HMW2A from retrievable text; absence from abstract is not counter-evidence.

results.resolved_title: Approaching a state shift in Earth's biosphere
results.quote: Here we review evidence that the global ecosystem as a whole can react in the same way and is approaching a planetary-scale critical transition as a result of human influence.
results.note: DOI 10.1038/nature11018 (PMID 22678279) is a Barnosky et al. ecology review on Earth's biosphere, not about Shigella OspI. The real OspI paper is Sanada 2012, 10.1038/nature10894. Wrong-DOI provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: Ats-1 Is Imported into Host Cell Mitochondria and Interferes with Apoptosis Induction
results.quote: we screened a genomic DNA library offor effectors of the type IV secretion system... A hypothetical protein was identified as a putative effector, hereby namedranslocatedubstrate 1 (Ats-1)
results.note: Ats-1 (APH_0859) is a T4SS effector of Anaplasma phagocytophilum, translocated into host cells (mitochondria).

results.resolved_title: TdeA, a TolC-like protein required for toxin and drug export in Actinobacillus actinomycetemcomitans
results.quote: produces leukotoxin (LtxA), a member of the RTX family of toxins... an outer membrane channel-forming protein, TolC, is required for toxin secretion... requires a TolC-like protein for LtxA secretion
results.note: Abstract (PMID 17116373). LtxA is an RTX (T1SS) toxin secreted via the TolC-like TdeA in A. actinomycetemcomitans.

results.resolved_title: Txc, a New Type II Secretion System of Pseudomonas aeruginosa Strain PA7, Is Regulated by the TtsS/TtsR Two-Component System and Directs Specific Secretion of the CbpE Chitin-Binding Protein
results.quote: we demonstrate that this Txc-secreted substrate binds chitin, and we therefore name it CbpE (chitin-binding protein E).
results.note: Abstract (PMID 24748613). CbpE (PSPA7_3026) is the substrate of the Txc T2SS in P. aeruginosa PA7.

results.resolved_title: Neisseria meningitidis causes cell cycle arrest of human brain microvascular endothelial cells at S phase via p21 and cyclin G2
results.quote: we analysed the effect of the human pathogen Neisseria meningitidis on cell cycle in a brain endothelial cell line as well as in primary brain endothelial cells.
results.note: DOI 10.1111/cmi.12482 (PMID 26149128) is a Neisseria meningitidis cell-cycle paper, not about Legionella pneumophila AnkB T4SS effector. Wrong-DOI provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: Dynamic remodeling of host membranes by self-organizing bacterial effectors
results.quote: Legionella translocates more than 300 effector proteins via the type 4 secretion system (T4SS) into the host cell... We analyzed the T4SS effectors and identified MavQ (Lpg2975)
results.note: MavQ (lpg2813/Lpg2975) is a Legionella pneumophila T4SS (Dot/Icm) effector translocated into host cells.

results.resolved_title: T6SS4 is heterogeneously expressed in Yersinia pseudotuberculosis and is a target for transcriptional and post-transcriptional regulation
results.quote: One of these potential effectors (YPK_3548) is encoded at the 3’-end of the T6SS4 cluster.
results.note: YPK_3548 named as a T6SS4 effector encoded in the T6SS4 cluster (Y. pseudotuberculosis). Paper focuses on T6SS4 regulation; effector status attributed to it directly and via cited prior work.

results.resolved_title: Contact-independent killing mediated by a T6SS effector with intrinsic cell-entry properties
results.quote: YPK_0954, which does not contain a PAAR domain but is located downstream of YPK_0952, is also a T6SS-3 effector
results.note: Tce1 (YPK_0954) is a T6SS-3-secreted nuclease effector (Y. pseudotuberculosis); abstract: 'uses a T6SS (T6SS-3) to secrete a nuclease effector that kills other bacteria'.

results.resolved_title: Intimin and Invasin Export Their C-Terminus to the Bacterial Cell Surface Using an Inverse Mechanism Compared to Classical Autotransport
results.quote: the N-terminal β-barrel domain acts as a translocation pore to secrete the C-terminal passenger domain... we propose that this class of autotransporters be termed Type Ve secretion.
results.note: Intimin (eae) is a Type Ve autotransporter (T5SS); its own N-terminal barrel secretes the C-terminal passenger to the cell surface in E. coli O157:H7.
